surface.name: Deepgram Playground no-signup transcription
surface.selection_basis: Under the workshop policy, Deepgram Playground takes priority: the official first-request guide explicitly says it makes an API request without code or signup.

alternatives_considered.name: Deepgram Playground
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Fundamentals guide presents this as a no-code, no-signup way to try the API rather than the authenticated developer API path.

alternatives_considered.name: Deepgram SDK route
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The page requires language-specific environment setup and then redirects developers to a product Getting Started guide.

alternatives_considered.name: Streaming Speech-to-Text
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: The Speech-to-Text overview presents streaming and pre-recorded audio as use-case choices; the Fundamentals cURL example selects a hosted pre-recorded file.

entry_point.developer_intent: Use Deepgram Playground without an account to submit audio and observe a transcript.
assumed_prior_state: No Deepgram account, project, API key, billing method, request, or transcript is assumed.
A shell with cURL and network access is available; no signup, credential creation, credit use, or API request was performed during research.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Make a no-signup request in Deepgram Playground.
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: Deepgram Playground processes audio and displays a transcript.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: Recognized text appears in the Playground's Transcript panel.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The first-request guide says Playground can make a request with no signup, and the live Playground exposes a Transcript panel.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: Visible transcript text is the first completed output of the accountless Playground route.

prerequisites.requirement: Create a Deepgram account before creating the first API key; the current signup transition is client-rendered and not publicly enumerated.
Create the first project API key in the Deepgram Console, assign its role and expiration settings, and copy its secret at the one-time display.
Have a shell with cURL for the published raw HTTP route.
The pay-as-you-go signup includes $200 credit and does not require a credit card for the first request.

primary_path.action: Open Deepgram's Make Your First API Request guide and select the Playground route.
primary_path.output: The no-code, no-signup route is identified.

primary_path.action: Open Deepgram Playground on a large-screen browser.
primary_path.output: The trial controls and Transcript panel load.
primary_path.success_signal: The Playground displays input, model, feature, and transcript controls.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: The site recommends widening small browser windows.

primary_path.action: Select or provide the audio input and retain the desired language, model, and feature settings.
primary_path.success_signal: The input and selected model are shown.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Available options differ between pre-recorded and live input.

primary_path.success_signal: The Playground begins listening or processing.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Wait for speech processing to complete.

primary_path.success_signal: The Transcript panel contains text from the submitted audio.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Check audio input and model settings if no transcript appears.

branches.path: Open Deepgram Playground, which the Fundamentals guide says can make a request without signup.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Avoids account and credential setup but changes the surface from an authenticated developer API request to a no-code trial.

branches.path: Configure the selected language environment, install its Deepgram SDK, and continue to a product-specific Getting Started guide.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds runtime and package setup and changes the execution route.

branches.condition: Transcribe a local file after account setup
branches.path: Send the audio bytes with the documented content type instead of submitting the hosted remote URL.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Adds local-file selection while preserving the transcription response terminal.

friction_gates.description: The mandatory signup application does not expose its form or transitions in the public official response.
The first API key must be created in the console, and its full secret is shown only once.
API-key creation requires choosing a role that controls the key's permitted actions.

sources.title: Make Your First API Request
sections_used: Make a Request to the API

sources.title: Creating API Keys
sections_used: Create an API key using the Deepgram Console

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields, identity-provider choices, terms, verification actions, and completed-account state appear in the current Deepgram Console?
uncertainties.impact: The required account portion cannot be reconstructed or counted.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The official guide links into a client-rendered application whose public response contains no signup states.

excluded_after_success: Streaming transcription, text-to-speech, Voice Agent, custom models, callbacks, production key rotation, and paid credit loading occur outside the documented first pre-recorded request.
